generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring sensor for occupancy detection within building spaces, providing real-time occupancy data for the Digital Twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring indoor temperature to support real-time operational monitoring and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring indoor humidity levels for comfort and system performance monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring real-time energy consumption of building systems (HVAC, lighting, etc.) for operational analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance EquipmentStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor providing real-time status of building equipment (e.g., HVAC units, pumps) for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralLoadSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring structural loads and vibrations for real-time structural health monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralLoadDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor
]

instance StructuralLoadDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralLoadSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational Building Information Model (BIM) containing geometric and spatial representations of building components, inherited from design phases."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDtTransformationEnabler
]

instance BcsBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Control System (BCS) behavioral model enriched with device-specific configurations (sensors, actuators, controllers) for operational simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BcsModelingEnabler
]

instance EnergySimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based energy simulation model derived from BIM and operational data for performance analysis and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergySimulationEnabler
]

instance StructuralAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structural analysis model for dynamic behavior simulation under operational loads, integrated with real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralSimulationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time data from IoT sensors and building management systems, synchronized with the Digital Twin for live system representation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted, StructuralLoadDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical operational data including maintenance logs, energy consumption records, and sensor archives for predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance BimMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Static metadata from BIM models including device specifications, control logic, and spatial relationships for semantic mapping."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDtTransformationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimToDtTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) framework for automated extraction of structured DT artifacts from BIM models with minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance BcsModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tooling for defining and configuring Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) within the BIM environment."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAndAutomationService
]

instance EnergySimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine (e.g., EnergyPlus) for building energy performance analysis and optimization using physics-based models."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance StructuralSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structural analysis tool (e.g., ANSYS) for dynamic behavior simulation under operational loads."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data ingestion and synchronization pipeline for consolidating disparate data streams into a unified operational model."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning framework for failure forecasting, energy demand prediction, and lifecycle cost optimization using historical and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance CollaborationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized data repository with version control and domain-specific ontologies for multi-disciplinary collaboration in AECO workflows."
    DTDFVocab:enables CollaborationService
]

instance IotDataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database (e.g., InfluxDB) and edge computing architecture for high-velocity IoT data handling with low-latency processing."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlAndAutomationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data ingestion and validation from IoT sensors and BMS to create a live virtual replica of the physical asset, enabling anomaly detection and performance benchmarking."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSystemStateInsight, EquipmentFailureAlertAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Machine learning-driven forecasting for predictive maintenance, energy demand prediction, and occupancy pattern analysis using historical and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureProbabilityInsight, EnergyDemandForecastInsight, MaintenanceScheduleRecommendationAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-objective optimization for energy efficiency, occupant comfort, and structural integrity via simulation-based what-if analysis and automated control adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationInsight, OccupantComfortOptimizationInsight, StructuralIntegrityAlertAction
]

instance ControlAndAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control integration with physical systems to execute automated adjustments based on DT insights, including fault mitigation and prescriptive control policies."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeCorrectiveActionAction, PrescriptiveControlPolicyAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D/5D modeling with interactive dashboards and AR/VR interfaces for scenario visualization and collaborative decision support."
    DTDFVocab:provides RefurbishmentImpactVisualizationInsight, FailurePropagationVisualizationInsight
]

instance CollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Centralized data repository with version control and access permissions for cross-disciplinary teams to resolve conflicts and reuse knowledge across projects."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignClashResolutionInsight, StandardizedDataExchangeAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeSystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live virtual replica of the physical asset showing current operational states, sensor readings, and system performance metrics."
]

instance EquipmentFailureAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert generation for detected equipment failures or anomalies in real-time operational data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailureProbabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Probabilistic model of equipment failure risks based on historical data and current operational states."
]

instance EnergyDemandForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted energy consumption patterns for the building, enabling proactive resource allocation."
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of maintenance schedules based on predictive analytics and equipment health metrics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized energy consumption strategies derived from simulation-based analysis of current and potential operational configurations."
]

instance OccupantComfortOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for adjusting environmental controls to maximize occupant comfort while maintaining energy efficiency."
]

instance StructuralIntegrityAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert for detected structural anomalies or load conditions exceeding safety thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RealTimeCorrectiveActionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated execution of corrective measures (e.g., fault mitigation, energy rerouting) based on real-time system state analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PrescriptiveControlPolicyAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of building control systems (e.g., HVAC setpoints) based on predictive and real-time insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RefurbishmentImpactVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization of potential refurbishment impacts on building performance and operations."
]

instance FailurePropagationVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of how equipment failures or operational changes propagate through building systems."
]

instance DesignClashResolutionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification and resolution of design clashes or conflicts between building systems during collaborative workflows."
]

instance StandardizedDataExchangeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated conversion and exchange of data between AECO systems using standardized formats (e.g., IFC, CityGML)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components (implied from services)
instance HvacActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building HVAC system actuator receiving control signals from the Digital Twin for dynamic adjustment of environmental conditions."
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building lighting system actuator controlled by the Digital Twin to optimize energy use based on occupancy and ambient light levels."
]

instance FaultMitigationActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated system for executing corrective actions in response to detected equipment failures or operational anomalies."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** in this DT system involves **control commands** generated by the digital twin and transmitted to physical building systems for execution. These commands include **actuation signals** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control, or security system triggers) and **configuration updates** (e.g., runtime adjustments to operational parameters). The **control message formats** are standardized to ensure compatibility with integrated services, though specific formats (e.g., JSON, MQTT payloads) are not detailed. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied to enforce security and prevent unauthorized modifications, aligning with the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** for interoperability and maintainability. **Feedback systems** provide confirmation of command execution (e.g., acknowledgment signals or status updates from physical devices), while **conflict resolution** is managed through the DT Framework’s **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. The system ensures **command execution confirmation** via bidirectional data flows, closing the loop between digital control and physical response."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the AECO domain requires **orchestration and coordination mechanisms** to integrate **static models** (e.g., BIM, CAD) with **runtime data** (e.g., IoT sensors, operational logs) into a unified cyber-physical system. The architecture must support **component integration** across heterogeneous data sources and models, leveraging **interoperability standards** (e.g., IFC, CityGML, or domain-specific ontologies) to ensure seamless data exchange. **Resource management** is critical, particularly in large-scale deployments, where computational and storage demands must be balanced with real-time performance requirements. The system-wide coordination relies on **predictive analytics and simulation engines** to enable data-driven decision-making, though the document does not specify exact orchestration frameworks (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or real-time synchronization protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components (e.g., functional requirements **F_X** in Table III). This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., executable models, simulation environments) with **minimal manual intervention**.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to synchronize the physical building and its digital twin, supported by **real-time data exchange capabilities** for continuous bidirectional synchronization.
  4. **Runtime Operation**: The DT operates in tandem with the physical infrastructure, enabling **interactive visualization** (immersive interfaces) and **modular service integration** (e.g., custom analytics, control logic extensions). Facility Managers interact with the twin for **monitoring and operational control**, while DT Engineers extend functionality via **custom services and plugins**.
  **Transition Management**: The framework ensures seamless transitions between phases through **standardized extension points** and **modular architecture**, allowing dynamic adaptation to evolving operational requirements. **Lifecycle governance** is implicit in the framework’s design, addressing theoretical and practical insights from research projects like **TwinLight and BOREALIS** (Section II)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework addresses **model fidelity** by bridging the gap between BIM’s **static representations** and the DT’s **dynamic operational requirements**, ensuring that enriched BIM models (with device-specific configurations) are accurately transformed into deployable DT artifacts. **Validation and verification** are implicit in the **automated generation process**, where structured representations are derived from BIM with minimal manual intervention, reducing human error. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between BIM models and real-world BCS components, though the document does not specify quantification methods. **Error detection and correction** are supported through **real-time data exchange**, enabling continuous synchronization between physical and digital systems. **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **modular architecture**, where standardized extension points allow for integration of custom validation services or analytics to monitor DT accuracy and operational integrity."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **dt_technical_connection** infrastructure is designed to support **heterogeneous system interoperability** and **real-time synchronization** between physical and digital components. The system employs a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling integration across diverse protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or proprietary IoT standards). The **network topology** is centralized, with the DT Framework acting as a **hub-and-spoke model** to facilitate bidirectional data exchange. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are optimized for low-overhead updates and real-time monitoring, though exact metrics (e.g., <100ms latency) are not specified. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are implied to address scalability and maintainability, ensuring continuous operation even during component failures. **Security protocols** for network communication are not explicitly detailed but are inferred to align with the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which emphasize accessibility and interoperability while mitigating risks in heterogeneous environments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **dt_hosting_deployment** infrastructure supports **multi-platform deployment models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** configurations, to accommodate varying operational needs. The system’s **computational and storage requirements** are dynamically scalable, enabling adjustments from individual components to entire infrastructure systems without full redeployment. The **deployment model** leverages the DT Framework’s **runtime configuration management**, allowing for modular service integration and low-overhead updates. **Availability and reliability** are assured through the framework’s **modular architecture** and **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which prioritize maintainability and fault tolerance. The deployment process begins with **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools to model devices and initiate deployment, followed by **Facility Managers** interacting with the operational DT for real-time monitoring. The system’s **scalability mechanisms** ensure seamless expansion as new services or analytics are integrated by **DT Engineers**, maintaining operational continuity throughout the building lifecycle."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system in the AECO domain must support **horizontal integration** with **external systems** through standardized **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability frameworks** to bridge fragmented models and data silos. Key dependencies include integration with **enterprise systems** (e.g., ERP, CMMS), **domain-specific tools** (e.g., BIM platforms like Revit or ArchiCAD), and **IoT/OT ecosystems** (e.g., sensor networks, PLCs). The document emphasizes the need for **cross-domain collaboration** and **real-time data synchronization**, though it does not specify exact protocols (e.g., REST APIs, GraphQL, or proprietary interfaces) or integration patterns (e.g., API gateways, middleware layers). The system’s effectiveness hinges on adhering to **industry standards** (e.g., ISO 19650, BIM Collaboration Format) to ensure compatibility with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems across the building lifecycle."
]
